benchmark_details.name: ChineseSafe: A Chinese Benchmark for Evaluating Safety in Large Language Models
benchmark_details.overview: ChineseSafe is a comprehensive safety benchmark for evaluating the safety of large language models in Chinese contexts, focusing on 205,034 examples across 4 classes and 10 sub-classes of safety issues.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the safety of large language models in identifying unsafe content in Chinese scenarios.

data.source: Collected from open-sourced datasets and internet resources.
data.annotation: Data processing includes cleaning and deduplication performed on the collected data.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on model predictions for identifying unsafe content.
methodology.interpretation: Higher scores indicate better performance in identifying unsafe content.
methodology.validation: Extensive experiments on various mainstream LLMs.
